title: nvm_list guards against ID=0 server response and u16 cursor wraparound
description: nvm.rs: the nvm_list loop uses WH_ERROR_NOTFOUND in out_rc as the normal end-of-list signal (not an error). It also breaks on out_id==0 (WH_NVM_ID_INVALID) even when out_rc is success, which would otherwise leave start_id stuck at 0 and produce an infinite loop on a misbehaving server. The cursor advance uses checked_add(1) and breaks on overflow rather than wrapping around and re-scanning IDs. A future contributor might remove the out_id==0 guard as 'defensive bloat' or replace checked_add with +1. The guard is correct: the C server protocol does not guarantee well-formed responses and an infinite loop here would hang the caller indefinitely. The overflow break is correct: u16 ID space is finite and wrap-around would revisit IDs.
